Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: I can see the character has been rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise. The feet are now on the LEFT side of the character. Let me check the exact positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"Step: {current_frame.step}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] != 'S':
        minr = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        minc = min(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={minr}-{maxr} cols={minc}-{maxc}")

green_total = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
red_total = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R')
print(f"\nGreen: {green_total}, Red: {red_total}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now I see:
- White body: rows 11-25, cols 14-28
- Red feet: rows 14-16, cols 11-13 and rows 20-22, cols 11-13 (on the LEFT side)
- Green frame: rows 29-49, cols 41-49
- Stationary red blocks: rows 35-37, cols 38-40 and rows 41-43, cols 38-40
The character has been rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise. The feet are now on the LEFT side at rows 14-16 and 20-22, cols 11-13.
The stationary red blocks are at rows 35-37 and 41-43, cols 38-40.
To align the feet with the stationary red blocks:
- Feet rows: 14-16 and 20-22 -> need to move DOWN to rows 35-37 and 41-43
- Feet cols: 11-13 -> need to move RIGHT to cols 38-40
Row difference: 35 - 14 = 21 rows DOWN = 7 DOWN moves (7 * 3 = 21)
Col difference: 38 - 11 = 27 cols RIGHT = 9 RIGHT moves (9 * 3 = 27)
But wait, if I move DOWN by 21 rows, the feet would be at rows 35-37 and 41-43, which matches the stationary red blocks. And if I move RIGHT by 27 cols, the feet would be at cols 38-40, which also matches.
But I need to be careful. The character's body is at rows 11-25, cols 14-28. If I move DOWN by 21 rows, the body would be at rows 32-46. If I move RIGHT by 27 cols, the body would be at cols 41-55. The green frame is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49. So the body would overlap with the green frame.
Let me try moving DOWN by 7 and RIGHT by 9 to align the feet with the stationary red blocks.
Actually wait, I need to reconsider. The feet are at rows 14-16 and 20-22 (two pairs). The stationary red blocks are at rows 35-37 and 41-43. The row difference between the first pair is 35 - 14 = 21 rows. The row difference between the second pair is 41 - 20 = 21 rows. So both pairs need to move DOWN by 21 rows = 7 DOWN moves.
The col difference is 38 - 11 = 27 cols = 9 RIGHT moves.
Let me try: 7 DOWN + 9 RIGHT = 16 moves. But this might cause game over if the character moves too far from the green frame. Let me be more careful.
Actually, the character is currently at rows 11-25, cols 14-28. The green frame is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49. The character is ABOVE and to the LEFT of the green frame. Moving DOWN and RIGHT would bring the character closer to the green frame, so it shouldn't cause game over.
Let me try: 7 DOWN + 9 RIGHT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: feet at rows 14-16 and 20-22, cols 11-13 (LEFT side)
# Stationary targets: rows 35-37 and 41-43, cols 38-40
# Need: DOWN by 21 rows (7 moves) + RIGHT by 27 cols (9 moves) = 16 moves total

# Let me batch all moves
moves = ['DOWN'] * 7 + ['RIGHT'] * 9
action(moves)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
